detector_only: you have been working on a deliverable for three days. your manager tells you it needs to be completely reworked. what is your first thought?
you are asked to format and proofread a report that someone else on the team wrote. what is your reaction?
it is your first week. you are asked to sit in on a client meeting and take notes. how do you feel about it?

tell us about something you got wrong on a work, internship, or school project -- a mistake, a missed deadline, a deliverable that had to be reworked. what happened, what did you do, and what was the actual consequence?
describe a specific time you caught an error or inconsistency in your own work or someone else's before it caused a problem. what did you find, what did you do about it, and what happened?
which of the following have you done in the past 12 months? select all that apply.
which best describes what energizes you most about the way you work?
which of these sounds most like the kind of work you want to do?
what are you most hoping to get out of this role?
what is your availability to start a new role?
where do you see yourself in 5 to 10 years professionally?
ai_only: By providing your phone number you agree to receive informational text messages from Hotman Group.
reasons: field count delta 11 exceeds tolerance 2 (detector=41, ai=30)

ai.notes: The form uses a dynamic questionnaire system. Some fields are conditionally rendered or trigger disqualification logic based on user input.

fields.question: Upload Resume
Full Name
Email Address
Phone Number
By providing your phone number you agree to receive informational text messages from Hotman Group.
Desired Salary
Salary Period
Experience Summary
Cover Letter
Are you legally authorized to work in the U.S. without employer sponsorship, now or in the future?
Will you ever require H1B visa sponsorship to work in the United States?
What city and state are you currently located in?
This position is fully remote. Describe your current home workspace.
options: I have a private room with a door I can close, used exclusively or primarily as my workspace
I have a dedicated desk or area but the room is shared and I cannot always close a door
I do not have a dedicated workspace -- I work wherever I happen to be
I typically work from coffee shops, libraries, or shared spaces outside my home

fields.question: Which Microsoft 365 applications do you use regularly? Select all that apply.
What is your expected hourly rate for this contract engagement?
If this role converts to a permanent full-time position, what are your annual salary expectations?
What is the highest level of education you have completed?
Which of the following cybersecurity or GRC certifications do you currently hold? Select all that apply.
Are you currently bound by a non-compete, non-solicitation, or similar agreement that could restrict your ability to work for Hotman Group or its clients?
Do you consent to a background check as a condition of employment or contract engagement?
Are you willing to provide professional references?
Tell us about your background and how you got interested in GRC and cybersecurity. What have you done so far, through school, work, internships, or on your own, that is relevant to this role?

Which GRC frameworks, security standards, or compliance concepts have you worked with or studied? For each one you list, describe what you actually did with it -- not just that you are familiar with it.

Describe a project, assignment, or work task you completed that you are proud of. What was it, what was your specific role, and what was one thing you had to figure out or work through to get it done?
Why are you applying to this role, and why Hotman Group specifically?
You finish an assigned task. What do you do?
options: Hold it until someone asks -- you do not want to send something before it has been fully reviewed
Send it to whoever assigned it and move on to the next thing
Do a final self-review to make sure it is solid, then send it
Let the relevant team members know it is done, summarize what was completed, and flag anything they should be aware of

fields.question: A client emails you with a question and you do not have a complete answer yet. What do you do?
options: Wait to respond until you have a complete, accurate answer
Acknowledge receipt and let them know when they can expect a full response
Forward it to your manager to handle
Reply and ask them to follow up if they have not heard from you in a few days

fields.question: You are working remotely and realize you accidentally sent a draft document containing client names to the wrong email address. What do you do first?
options: Delete it from your sent folder and then email the recipient asking them to disregard it
Email the recipient and ask them to delete it without mentioning it to anyone internally
Wait to see if the recipient responds before deciding how to handle it

fields.question: You are reviewing a list of risk items a client maintains. You notice the risk scores have been reduced across the board but you cannot find any record of new controls being added or existing ones being improved. What do you do first?
options: Accept the scores as current -- the client manages their own risk register
Ask the client directly what changed to justify the lower scores
Cross-reference the scores against available documentation before drawing any conclusion, then flag it to your manager
Note it as a minor observation and move on

fields.question: It is Friday afternoon and a client sends an urgent message saying a laptop with sensitive information was stolen. They are asking what to do. You are the only one available. What do you do first?
options: Tell them to contain the situation immediately, have the employee confirm no further sharing occurred, document everything they know right now, and let them know you are looping in your team as soon as possible
Ask them a series of clarifying questions to fully understand the situation before saying anything
Walk them through what you know about data breach response and help them start assessing the situation
Tell them you will have someone follow up with them first thing Monday
